Cambridge City
MA

City Manager's Agenda Item
CMA 2025 #286

A communication transmitted from Yi-An Huang, City Manager, relative to Awaiting Report Item Number 25-63, which requested public open-house meetings with Eversource regarding heat and electricity bills.

Information

Department:City Manager's OfficeSponsors:
Category:Awaiting Report Response

Links

OriginAR-25-63 : That the City Manager is requested to reach out to representatives from Eversource to coordinate at least four public open-house meetings in the coming weeks regarding heat and electricity costs.
Originating Order

Attachments

Version History
  1. Printout

Body

 

 

 

To the Honorable, the City Council:

In response to Awaiting Report 25-63 which requested the City coordinate public open-house meetings with Eversource regarding heat and electricity bills, we report the following.

 

A cross-departmental team – including staff from the Department of Public Works (DPW), the Office of Sustainability, the Department of Human Service Programs (DHSP) Fuel Assistance Division and  Council on Aging – is actively organizing a series of open-house events to help residents save energy and lower their utility bills.

 

These meetings are designed to allow residents to meet directly with City staff and Eversource representatives and to learn more about resources that help lower energy use and costs.. The first open house is scheduled for December 15, 2025 at the Cambridge Senior Center in Central Square, with additional meetings to follow.  These efforts are part of the City’s broader commitment to equitable access to energy education and support, particularly for residents in high-need communities.

 

In addition to the open houses, the Office of Sustainability provides year-round education and support to help Cambridge residents better understand their energy bills and take action to reduce energy costs. This includes:

 

·         Live support through the Cambridge Energy Helpline (617-430-6230)

·         Energy Bill Check-Ups to help residents understand their bills and identify cost-saving programs

·         Workshops and presentations for residents and staff of community organizations

·         A full list of available programs is at https://www.cambridgema.gov/sustainable/

 

Additional outreach efforts from the Office of Sustainability in November and December include:

 

·         Presentations at the Cambridge Public Library and to the Community Art Center’s PortARISE group

·         Energy bill education events at Inman Square Apartments and 808–812 Memorial Drive

·         Drop-in Energy Bill Check-Ups for patrons of the Finch Food Pantry at 675 Concord Avenue

 

In regards to heating assistance, the DHSP’s Fuel Assistance Office administers the federal Home Energy Assistance Program (HEAP). HEAP helps low-income residents in Cambridge and Somerville pay winter heating costs incurred between November 1 and April 30.

 

Beginning each fall, staff send targeted mailings to 1,800+ previous HEAP applicants to support them in applying for the 2025-26 winter season.  They also conduct a thorough outreach strategy to engage new applicants.

 

 

 

 

This includes partnering with Cambridge Public Schools, the City’s Community Engagement Teams, the Council on Aging, the Commission for Persons with Disabilities, and other community organizations to share information with eligible, low-income households and residents from historically underserved communities in Cambridge. Outreach materials are distributed in ten languages.

 

Fuel Assistance staff collaborate with the Office of Sustainability and other partners to connect HEAP clients to additional low-cost, energy-saving programs offered by the Commonwealth and other agencies. The program also provides assistance within 24-hours for eligible HEAP clients experiencing heating emergencies.

 

Staff from the Fuel Assistance Office will be available at open houses to provide information about HEAP and help eligible residents fill out an application on-site.

 

We appreciate the Council’s continued support in ensuring that residents are informed and engaged. We will continue to provide updates as additional open house dates and locations are confirmed. We welcome your feedback and recommendations for reaching additional residents who would benefit from our work.

 

 

Very truly yours,

 

 

 

 

 

Yi-An Huang

City Manager
